question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing that CPI increased only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. The market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty since the actual data is now available.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI rose only 0.3%
This is well below the 0.9% threshold
Market closes tomorrow but data is already released
No possibility of revision that would change outcome significantly
risks: Potential data revision could occur but extremely unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.9%
Market timing - some participants may not be aware data was released
